qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ASGI file upload limit bypass keeps oversized uploads in memory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a PostGIS RasterField SQL injection caused by unsafe raster band index handling during raster lookup SQL construction, while this finding is an ASGI multipart upload buffering issue caused by trusting understated Content-Length for in-memory upload handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's RasterField/PostGIS band-index SQL injection root cause. The only qualifying finding is a separate ASGI upload memory-threshold bypass in a different subsystem with a different sink and impact class.
evidence: GHSA-mwm9-4648-f68q describes SQL injection in Django RasterField lookups on PostGIS via the band index parameter.

The upstream fix for CVE-2026-1207 adds integer type checks for lookup.band_lhs and lookup.band_rhs in django/contrib/gis/db/backends/postgis/operations.py before those band indices are injected into raster lookup SQL templates.

The only qualifying finding's report and reproduced PoC exercise the ASGI upload path by understating Content-Length so an oversized multipart upload remains an InMemoryUploadedFile despite FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE.
No qualifying finding report, reproduction artifact, or PoC evidence references RasterField, PostGIS raster lookups, band index handling, or SQL query construction.
